roof_system: Predominantly smooth low-slope membrane roof, likely built-up or modified bitumen, with a separate smooth low-slope field that may be a coated membrane

possible_roof_systems.evidence: Large flat roof fields with uniform tan/brown surfacing and perimeter flashing lines are consistent with a smooth low-slope bituminous roof finish.
Some areas show a muted, weathered rooftop texture typical of aged low-slope bituminous assemblies, though gravel is not clearly resolved at this aerial distance.
The right-side roof field has a relatively uniform light pink/tan appearance that could indicate a reflective coating over an existing low-slope membrane.
The image shows broad low-slope membrane fields, but the color and texture are not diagnostic enough to confirm black EPDM; only a generic membrane possibility.

roof_age_estimate: Likely original or older low-slope commercial roofing, with visible materials and layout consistent with an aging roof from the building era; exact age not determinable from aerial imagery alone.

notes: Several darkened roof areas and tonal variation are visible across the large left roof field, which may reflect weathering, staining, or prior repairs.
The left roof field contains numerous skylights/penetrations, increasing flashing and leak risk on an older low-slope roof.
Multiple rooftop units, vents, and curbs are visible across the property, adding serviceability and flashing complexity.
No obvious tree overhang or leaf accumulation is visible in this aerial image.

observations: Large industrial warehouse complex with extensive low-slope roof area.
Left roof field shows many evenly spaced skylights/penetrations and multiple rooftop appurtenances.
Darkened patches and uneven coloration are visible on the left roof field, especially near seams and across broad areas.
Right roof field appears more uniform and lighter in color, possibly a coated or newer membrane field.
Perimeter edges and transitions show multiple flashing conditions, but fine-detail seal condition cannot be confirmed from aerial imagery.
summary: Aerial imagery dated 03/11/2018 - 03/17/2018 shows a large low-slope commercial roof complex in fair condition with moderate serviceability risk. The roof appears to be a smooth membrane or bituminous system, with one field showing broad dark discoloration and tonal variation that may indicate weathering, prior repairs, or moisture-related staining. Numerous skylights, vents, and rooftop units increase flashing and leak potential. No clear tree overhang or debris buildup
recommendation: Have a qualified commercial roof-coating contractor perform an onsite inspection and moisture testing where warranted to confirm the roof system, identify required repairs, and verify restoration eligibility. If the substrate is sound and the roof is otherwise suitable, evaluate a silicone roof restoration before a more costly overlay or replacement; a properly designed silicone system can reduce tear-off waste, cost, and disruption, create a seamless monolithic white reflective cool-roof surface, withstand ponding water when properly installed, improve UV and hail resistance, and may qualify for a manufacturer warranty of up to 20 years. If onsite findings show widespread moisture, failed insulation, incompatible substrate, or advanced deterioration, then an overlay or replacement evaluation is more appropriate.
